results.title: A room for Romeo Brass
results.creator_credit: Shane Meadows, Charles Pattinson, George Faber, Alliance Atlantis and BBC Film Unit
results.brief_description: Shane Meadows writes and directs this drama about two young boys’ friendship and their coming-of-age. Neighbours, the boys share life experiences (and porno magazines) and support each other as one is physically unwell and the other’s parents have separated. The friendship is tested however when a creepy stranger manipulates his way into their lives by trying to date the attractive older sister of Romeo. Tensions arise as the stranger becomes more violent as his affections are rejected. Touching, funny and disturbing, this feature stars Paddy Considine as the stranger, with wonderful performances by the two young leads Andrew Shim and Ben Marshall. Bob Hoskins makes an appearance as a visiting teacher. Soundtrack features music by Beck, Beth Orton and Billy Bragg.

results.title: The Story of the weeping camel = Die Geschichte vom weinenden kamel
results.creator_credit: Byambasuren Davaa, Luigi Falorni, Tobias Siebert, Film Munchen and Hochschule fur Fernsehen
results.brief_description: Set in the Gobi Desert, Southern Mongolia, this narrative-style documentary explains a wonderful custom that could be used as an allegory for motherhood in the modern world. Made by two student filmmakers, they travel to Mongolia to meet a family of three generations living a simple nomadic lifestyle. During the spring, one of the family’s camels has a difficult delivery, giving birth to a rare white colt but she then rejects the youngster. Two family members set off to fetch a musician who plays a violin-like stringed instrument. The musician and a female family member perform a simple ritual that will re-unite the mother camel with her young. Striking landscape vistas and moving insight into the nomadic family life are captured by Luigi Falorni’s cinematography in this poignant representation of folklore and traditional storytelling. Nominated in the 2004 Academy Awards for Best Documentary (feature length).
results.creator_credit_for_label: Byambasuren Davaa, Luigi Falorni, Tobias Siebert, Film Munchen and Hochschule fur Fernsehen

results.title: Casa de los babys
results.creator_credit: John Sayles, Alejandro Springall, Lemore Syvan, Blue Magic Pictures, Springall Pictures and Syvan
results.brief_description:
Low key effort from esteemed director John Sayles, who in this ensemble drama, explores the issues of adoption of South American babies by Northern American women. The story centres around six women who are impatiently passing time, awaiting a child to take home with them. Each character, filled with anticipation (and some with fear), discusses their new role as mother, reflecting upon their pasts, revealing key moments that lead them to this point. Sayles also devotes some narrative time to the story of a young impoverished boy and his depressing milieu, a pregnant teenager and her overbearing mother, a cleaning woman raising her siblings and a hotel manager and her errant son. Multiple story threads dilutes the emotional impact, but fine acting and some sticky social issues provide the film with occasional potency.
Cast includes: Daryl Hannah, Lili Taylor, Maggie Gyllenhaal, Mary Steenburgen

results.title: Go now
results.creator_credit: Michael Winterbottom, Andrew Eaton, David M. Thompson and Revolution Studios
results.brief_description: Tackling the themes of chronic illness and relationships this relatively early work from Michael Winterbottom chronicles a young man’s path from an affable lucky-in-love bloke, to an irascible invalid. Along the way he tests the patience of his loyal girlfriend as he struggles to come to grips with the disease, his diminished physicality and his place within his working class milieu as man who does not work, and who, at a young age, faces an uncertain future. Wonderful plebeian Scottish brogue by screenplay writers Paul Henry Powell and Jimmy McGovern.

results.title: Festival express
results.creator_credit: Bob Smeaton, Gavin Poolman, John Trapman, Apollo Films and Peachtree Films
results.brief_description: In the summer of 1970, two Canadian music promoters decided to put on a touring music festival. Instead of the artists meeting at each location, they put everyone on a train that went from city to city. What a ride! This documentary is a re-discovery of the musical journey that took place and for some the great emotional journey from the vibe and friendship/good times that happened on the train. Never before had performers been so well looked after and the train became a place for one hell of party - Buddy Guy says at one point he tried to sleep but was worried he might miss something - one car was for blues jamming, one for folk, one for food and one for, well, other activities. The whole event ran at a great loss, word on the street was that the concerts should be free and youth of the day would charge the gates causing trouble at every town. That is why this footage was not uncovered for more that 30 years, no one had any money at the end of it to produce the film and it just sat in a vault! Some split-screen technique is used combining contemporary interviews with original footage or simply showing the two points-of-view of a two camera shot. Performers featured are: “The Band”, “Delaney and Bonnie”, “The Flying Burrito Brothers”, “Grateful Dead”, “Buddy Guy Blues Band”, “Ian & Sylvia & The Great Speckled Bird”, “Janis Joplin”, “Mashmakhan” and “Sha Na Na”. Cities visited include Toronto, Winnipeg and Calgary (with a stop at a liquor store to re-stock!). Two disc set contains lots of extras.

results.title: Outrageous fortune
results.creator_credit: Arthur Hiller, Robert W. Cort, Ted Field, Interscope communications and Touchstone Pictures
results.brief_description: A madcap comedy involving a menage a trois and espionage. Two actresses who don’t get along with each other, Sandy (Bette Midler) and Lauren (Shelley Long) find out they are both dating the same man, Michael (Peter Coyote). All hell breaks loose when they discover that Michael is a spy working for a foreign government and that he has implicated them both in his schemes. Cast includes Robert Prosky, John Schuck, George Carlin.

results.title: Ruthless people
results.creator_credit: David Zucker, Jerry Zucker, Jim Abrahams, Michael Peyser, Silver Screen Partners II, Touchstone Pictures and Wagner Productions
results.brief_description: A black comedy tale of two kindly kidnappers, Ken and Sandy Kessler, exacting revenge on the rip-off merchant Sam (Danny DeVito) by taking his spoilt rich wife, Barbara (Bette Midler) as hostage. However when the Kesslers try to claim their ransom, the obnoxious and spiteful Sam, who is delighted at the opportunity to be rid of the wife he hates so much, refuses to pay. Barbara joins forces with the Kesslers and together they plan their revenge. Cast includes Judge Reinhold, Helen Slater, Anita Morris and Bill Pullman in his debut performance.
results.creator_credit_for_label: David Zucker, Jerry Zucker, Jim Abrahams, Michael Peyser, Silver Screen Partners II, Touchstone Pictures and Wagner Productions

results.title: Miami rhapsody
results.creator_credit: David Frankel, Barry Jossen and Hollywood Pictures
results.brief_description: This hilariously sexy comedy has critics everywhere standing and cheering! The irresistible Sarah Jessica Parker (Honeymoon in Vegas, L.A. story) stars as Gwyn Marcus - a recently-engaged young woman about to make a shockingly funny discovery: marriage and fidelity don’t always go hand-in-hand, especially in her own family! From then on, it’s comical chaos as one by one, Gwyn unravels the details of their outrageously tangled affairs. Stars Sarah Jessica Parker, Mia Farrow, and Antonio Banderas.

results.title: Six days seven nights
results.creator_credit: Ivan Reitman, Roger Birnbaum, Wallis Nicita and Touchstone Pictures
results.brief_description: The perfect scenario - a remote island vacation with the man you love. He proposes, you say yes, life is wonderful. Then you are called away to do a one-day shoot in Tahiti and suddenly your life changes. High-powered New York magazine editor Robin Monroe (Anne Heche) finds herself in this very situation when the plane, engaged for the one-day shoot and piloted by the rough and ready Quinn Harris (Harrison Ford), is forced to land in stormy weather on an unknown remote island. Robin and Quinn are left to deal with each other, with survival and an uncertain future, whilst Robin’s boyfriend Frank (David Schwimmer) and Quinn’s girlfriend Angelica (Jacqueline Obradors) share their fears and concern at the hotel bar. Filmed on location on the Hawaiian island of Kauai. Cast also includes Allison Janney, Temuera Morrison.

results.title: Inspector Gadget
results.creator_credit: David Kellogg, Andy Heyward, Jordan Kerner, Roger Birnbaum, Caravan Pictures and Walt Disney Productions
results.brief_description: A remake of the popular children’s animated TV series, this family oriented comedy, traces Gadget’s history as a mild-mannered security guard, reborn as super-detective, Inspector Gadget, able to foil criminals with his innumerable tools - which due to a freak incident - are now a part of his body. Gadget seeks to bring down the evil Dr Claw, (played with charm by Rupert Everett) - the man responsible for his transformation. Also stars Joely Fisher as Gadget’s romantic interest and Matthew Broderick as Inspector Gadget.
